title: Refresh agent skills: full worktree-isolation port from agent-workflow
description:
## Summary
coding-tool's .claude/ sits at agent-workflow commit e1bdcba (older simple branch-based flow). Port the canonical latest /plan + /work workflow and supporting skills from jdelfino/agent-workflow (the clean project-agnostic source; NOT eval, which is a project-specific downstream fork). Decision: full worktree-isolation port.

## Source
Clone jdelfino/agent-workflow; reconcile against existing .claude/. agent-workflow's AGENTS.md, and commands epic.md/gh-issue.md/merge.md/plan.md are already byte-identical to coding-tool's -> no change needed there.

## ADD (new)
- .claude/hooks/ (all 6 from agent-workflow): ask-on-main-edit.sh, block-branch-switch.sh, block-hook-bypass.sh, block-stale-worktree.sh, mark-stale-worktree.sh, worktree-create.sh.
  CUSTOMIZE worktree-create.sh's per-project setup block for this Node repo (symlink to the main install, install-as-needed beyond that):
    ln -s "$MAIN_REPO/node_modules" "$WORKTREE_PATH/node_modules"
    cp "$MAIN_REPO/.env.local" "$WORKTREE_PATH/.env.local"
- .claude/skills/test-runner/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/rebase/SKILL.md (copy verbatim).
- .claude/commands/bug.md and .claude/commands/fire.md (copy verbatim).

## OVERWRITE (bring to canonical)
- skills: coordinator, implementer, merge-queue, planner, reviewer-architecture, reviewer-correctness, reviewer-plan, reviewer-tests.
- command: work.md (worktree version).

## settings.json
Merge agent-workflow's hooks block (SessionStart + mark-stale-worktree; WorktreeCreate; PreToolUse: block-stale-worktree, block-branch-switch, block-hook-bypass, ask-on-main-edit) into .claude/settings.json, PRESERVING coding-tool's extra permission allows (npm, npx, sed).

## .gitignore
Add: .claude/worktrees/  and  .claude/.stale-worktree

## Deliberately NOT ported (keep project-agnostic)
- GitHub-App identity infra (scripts/setup-github-app.sh, generate-github-app-token.sh, .github/workflows/human-review-gate.yml) -> requires shared secret; conflicts with zero-shared-secrets. (Not in .claude/ anyway.)
- playwright-debugging skill -> coding-tool already has a repo-tailored e2e-testing skill covering the same ground; keep e2e-testing to avoid duplication (fold in any clearly-generic improvement only).
- Anything eval-specific (debug-prod, refactor-finder, eval infra).

## Verify
bash -n every .claude/hooks/*.sh; settings.json is valid JSON; ported SKILL.md frontmatter well-formed; 'git worktree add' + the node_modules symlink works.
close_reason: Ported canonical agent-workflow skills + worktree hooks, then folded in eval's generic improvements (translated to npm/jest/tsc/Supabase). Commits 37550bd, 3e8a106.

title: Strip 1Password/external secrets from devcontainer -> local-only dev
description:
## Summary
Remove all 1Password / external-secret dependencies from the devcontainer so it boots with no host 'op' install, no service account, and no shared vault. Secrets come entirely from the local Supabase stack.

## Files to modify
- .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
- .devcontainer/setup-secrets.sh   (rewrite)
- .devcontainer/post-create.sh     (trim)
- .gitignore

## Files to DELETE
- .devcontainer/init-1password.sh
- .devcontainer/install-1password-cli.sh
- .devcontainer/setup.sh
- .env.1password
- .devcontainer/ops/              (entire dir: prod-ops devcontainer, inherently 1Password/prod-coupled)

## Steps
1. devcontainer.json: remove the 'initializeCommand' line (./.devcontainer/init-1password.sh). Keep postCreateCommand + postStartCommand, features (docker-in-docker, supabase-cli, node, python, github-cli), node_modules volume, forwarded ports.
2. Rewrite .devcontainer/setup-secrets.sh (postStart), local-only, NO op/envsubst/op inject:
   a. supabase start   (idempotent)
   b. eval "$(supabase status -o env | grep -E '^(ANON_KEY|SERVICE_ROLE_KEY|API_URL)=')"
   c. Write .env.local DIRECTLY:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:54321
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=$ANON_KEY
      SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY=$SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
      SYSTEM_ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@test.local
      GEMINI_API_KEY=            (empty -> AI walkthrough stays disabled, expected)
      UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=    (empty -> rate limiting off)
      UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=
   NOTE: the app reads NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY (verified via grep process.env over src/), so ANON_KEY/SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from 'supabase status' are mapped onto those names. Confirm at runtime the local app accepts them.
3. post-create.sh: drop the install-1password-cli.sh and setup.sh calls. KEEP: beads install + 'bd hooks install', Claude Code install, 'npm install', playwright install, install-nsjail.sh, node_modules chown.
4. .gitignore: remove the '.op-token', '.op-vault', '.gh-token' lines (no longer generated). (Worktree ignores are added by the skills task.)
5. KEEP .env as-is (committed file with valid static local keys; harmless fallback overridden by .env.local). KEEP install-nsjail.sh (real local sandbox, not 1pw-related).

## Verify
Clean flow with NO op present: supabase start -> run setup-secrets.sh -> .env.local written with non-empty publishable/secret keys + correct URL. grep repo for op://, OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN, init-1password, .op-token -> no live references.
close_reason: Implemented: devcontainer is local-only (no 1Password); dead ops tooling removed

title: Turn coding-tool into a self-contained classroom teaching repo
description:
## Summary
Repurpose this repo as a classroom teaching repo: a trainee boots the devcontainer + Claude Code (local VS Code Dev Containers) and runs a /plan + /work feature exercise in 1-2 hours with ZERO external accounts or shared secrets. Today the devcontainer hard-requires 1Password (op on the host, a service account, a shared vault) just to get secrets, which blocks classroom use; the .claude/ agent skills are ~4 commits behind canonical jdelfino/agent-workflow.

## Scope (subtasks)
1. Strip 1Password / external secrets -> local-only dev.
2. Refresh agent skills: full worktree-isolation port from jdelfino/agent-workflow.
3. Classroom hardening: clean per-attendee Claude config + gh auth login as the only manual step.
4. End-to-end verification (no external creds).
Plus a deferred follow-up: pre-baked devcontainer image published to ghcr.

## Decisions (locked with maintainer)
- Source of truth for skills = jdelfino/agent-workflow (clean canonical template). NOT eval (eval is a project-specific downstream fork). coding-tool .claude currently sits at agent-workflow commit e1bdcba.
- Full worktree-isolation port (hooks + worktree workflow), not the older branch-based flow.
- GitHub auth = per-attendee 'gh auth login' with their own account + fork/use-as-template. NO shared credential, GitHub App, or PAT. agent-workflow's GitHub-App identity infra stays NOT ported.
- Delete .devcontainer/ops/ (prod-ops devcontainer; inherently 1Password/prod-coupled).
- Pre-baked image work is DEFERRED to its own issue.
- Run target = local VS Code Dev Containers + Claude Code CLI (not Codespaces, not the VS Code plugin).

## Work on a branch; keep changes reviewable; verify end-to-end before finishing.

title: Remove dead Vercel (remote) integration; keep local nsjail backend
description:
## Summary
The maintainer decommissioned the remote Vercel + Supabase projects, so all Vercel-related code/config is dead and should be removed from the teaching repo (don't want students wandering to dead remote projects). The app's code-execution uses a backend registry; the LOCAL nsjail path (src/server/code-execution/backends/local-python-backend.ts) MUST remain as the sole backend. This is a careful, test-touching removal — do it with TDD (tests green after).

## Remove
- Backend + wiring: src/server/code-execution/backends/vercel-sandbox-backend.ts (+ its __tests__), and de-register it from the registry/executor (registry.ts, backends/index.ts, executor-service.ts, ephemeral-execute.ts, index.ts, interfaces.ts as needed). Update affected tests (registry.test.ts, executor-service.test.ts, ephemeral-execute.test.ts, interfaces.test.ts, src/app/api/execute/__tests__/route.test.ts, src/app/api/sessions/[id]/route.ts).
- Dependency: '@vercel/sandbox' from package.json (+ lockfile).
- Env handling: VERCEL_SANDBOX_ENABLED, VERCEL_TOKEN, VERCEL_PROJECT_ID, VERCEL_TEAM_ID, VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN references.
- Deploy/CI/scripts/docs: vercel.json, .github/workflows/sandbox-integration.yml, scripts/test-vercel-sandbox.ts, docs/deployment/vercel-rate-limiting.md.
- Audit hardcoded decommissioned project refs (*.supabase.co) in supabase/config.toml and docs; remove/neutralize ones that point at the dead remote project (do NOT break generic auth URL validation in src/server/auth/supabase-provider.ts — verify first).

## Keep
- nsjail / local-python backend as the default and only execution backend.
- DISABLE_SANDBOX handling for tests/CI as appropriate.

## Verify
npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test all green; npm run dev still executes code via the local backend. grep repo for 'vercel'/'Vercel' -> only incidental/historical mentions remain.

## Discovered-from
coding-6aa. Maintainer directive: remote Supabase/Vercel projects are decommissioned.
close_reason: Removed dead Vercel sandbox backend (conservative scope). Deleted vercel-sandbox-backend + tests, logger.ts, @vercel/sandbox, vercel.json, sandbox-integration CI, test script, rate-limiting doc. Stripped Vercel wiring from registry/index/executor/ephemeral + tests; local-python is sole real backend (disabled fallback). Kept generic stateful machinery inert per user direction. lint+tsc+full suite green (3416); live exec verified (executeEphemeral -> local-python -> python3 -> '42'). 0 vercel refs remain.

title: Set up lefthook quality gates (pre-commit/pre-push) in coding-tool
description:
## Summary
coding-tool has no git-hook quality-gate runner today (no lefthook config, not a dependency; only 'bd hooks install' wires beads). eval/agent-workflow enforce lint/test/typecheck/secret-scan via lefthook, and the ported '.claude/hooks/block-hook-bypass.sh' (from coding-6aa.2) blocks attempts to bypass hooks, on the assumption such hooks exist. Set up lefthook here so the classroom repo has the same guardrails.

## Why
- A trainee's /work run should get fast local feedback (lint/type/test) and be blocked from committing secrets, same as the canonical workflow.
- Without it, block-hook-bypass.sh guards a bypass of hooks that do not exist.

## Files to add
- lefthook.yml at repo root
- add 'lefthook' to package.json devDependencies
- install + enable in .devcontainer/post-create.sh (e.g. 'npx lefthook install' after npm install)

## Suggested gates (map to existing package.json scripts)
- pre-commit (parallel, scoped by glob): eslint (staged src/**/*.{ts,tsx}); typecheck via 'npx tsc --noEmit'; gitleaks secret scan (keeps the no-secrets promise); plus the beads side via 'bd hooks run pre-commit' or 'bd hooks install'.
- pre-push (parallel): jest via 'npm test'. Integration/e2e stay in CI.

## Notes
- Reconcile with beads' own hooks so they coexist (lefthook can call 'bd hooks run <stage>').
- Keep it lightweight for a 1-2h classroom exercise: fast gates only; heavy suites stay in CI.

## Discovered-from
coding-6aa (classroom teaching repo epic); pairs with coding-6aa.2 (block-hook-bypass.sh).
notes: When lefthook is set up, also FLIP the skills' gate-running references back to 'enforced by hooks': the eval-port pass set coordinator/implementer/reviewer-correctness/rebase to 'the test-runner/coordinator is the gate (no git hooks)'. Once hooks exist, those should say pre-commit/pre-push hooks enforce lint/typecheck/tests. Files: .claude/skills/{coordinator,implementer,reviewer-correctness,rebase}/SKILL.md.
close_reason: Added lefthook quality gates. lefthook.yml owns all git hooks and delegates beads stages via 'bd hooks run' (pre-commit export, prepare-commit-msg trailers, pre-push, post-checkout, post-merge). Gates: pre-commit = eslint(staged) + tsc + gitleaks(skip-if-absent); pre-push = npm test. Added lefthook devDep; post-create.sh installs gitleaks binary (Go-free) + 'npx lefthook install' and no longer runs 'bd hooks install'. Flipped skill wording (coordinator/implementer/reviewer-correctness/rebase) from 'no git hooks' to hook-enforced. Verified via 'lefthook run pre-commit/pre-push': all gates green.

title: End-to-end verification: local-only boot, app serves, tests pass, no 1pw residue
description:
## Summary
Verify the classroom repo works end-to-end with NO external creds. A full devcontainer rebuild can't be done from the agent session, so verify the logic directly in the clone, then describe the exact steps a trainee runs.

## Steps
1. Secrets flow: install supabase CLI, 'supabase start' in the repo, run the new setup-secrets.sh, confirm .env.local has non-empty NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY + SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY and URL http://127.0.0.1:54321 -- with NO op/1Password present.
2. App serves: npm install -> npm run dev -> app boots and talks to local Supabase (no auth/key errors).
3. Tests pass: npm test (jest) green with no external creds.
4. No 1Password residue: grep repo for op://, OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN, init-1password, .op-token -> only intended/historical references remain.
5. Hooks sanity: bash -n .claude/hooks/*.sh; worktree add + node_modules symlink works; settings.json valid JSON.
6. Skills load: SKILL.md frontmatter well-formed.

## Done when
All six pass and the trainee-facing run/test steps are documented (README/SETUP). Depends on the secrets + skills tasks.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end local-only: secrets flow writes .env.local (local URL + non-empty keys, no 1pw), app serves (200 + Supabase auth 401, no key errors), npm test green (3459) with no external creds, no 1Password residue (copier dropped), hooks + skills sane. Timing report added to DEVCONTAINER.md. Filed coding-6aa.7 (untrack .env).

title: Classroom hardening: clean per-attendee Claude config + gh auth docs
description:
## Summary
Make first boot clean and reliable, and make 'gh auth login' the only manual step. (Supabase image pre-bake is a separate deferred issue.)

## Files to modify
- .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
- README.md, docs/SETUP.md, docs/DEVCONTAINER.md

## Steps
1. devcontainer.json: replace the host ~/.claude BIND-MOUNT with a named VOLUME so each attendee gets a clean, container-local Claude config (no host inheritance; survives restarts). Before:
     source=${localEnv:HOME}/.claude,target=/home/vscode/.claude,type=bind,consistency=cached
   After:
     source=coding-tool-claude-config,target=/home/vscode/.claude,type=volume
2. Docs: rewrite the 1Password sections of docs/DEVCONTAINER.md and docs/SETUP.md to the local-only flow (no op, no vault, no service account). Document 'gh auth login' (own account + fork / use-as-template) as the single manual step, only needed for the exercise PR. Update README setup section to match.
3. NO shared credential / GitHub App / PAT. No host gh token copied in. (main is already branch-protected upstream.)

## Verify
grep docs + README for 1Password/op residue; confirm devcontainer.json mount swap is valid JSON.
close_reason: Named-volume Claude config; docs rewritten local-only; gh auth login documented as the only manual step. Commit pending.

title: Untrack committed .env (redundant with generated .env.local)
description:
## Summary
`.env` is committed/tracked AND listed in `.gitignore` (tracked because it predates the ignore rule). It holds local Supabase defaults (URL + the public local-demo keys — not real secrets). It is effectively redundant in the local-only classroom flow and the dual tracked+ignored state is confusing.

## Evidence (from coding-6aa.4 verification)
- `git ls-files --error-unmatch .env` → tracked, while `.gitignore:15` lists `.env`.
- Jest uses the `ts-jest` preset (NOT `next/jest`), so it does NOT auto-load `.env`; `src/setupTests.ts` injects no Supabase env. `npm test` passed with no `.env.local` present, so tests/CI (`ci.yml` has no env step) do NOT depend on `.env`.
- `next dev` loads both `.env` and `.env.local` ("Environments: .env.local, .env"), but `setup-secrets.sh` writes `.env.local`, which overrides `.env`. So the app ignores the committed values anyway.

## Options
1. `git rm --cached .env` and delete it (rely on generated `.env.local`). Verify `npm test`, `npm run dev`, and CI still pass. Lowest-noise; matches "keys generated on boot, nothing committed".
2. Keep it but rename to a clearly-intentional `.env.defaults`-style file and remove `.env` from `.gitignore` to end the tracked+ignored contradiction.

## Done when
`.env` is no longer both tracked and gitignored; `npm test` + `npm run dev` + CI remain green.

title: Follow-up: pre-baked devcontainer image published to ghcr (fast first boot)
description:
## Summary
Deferred from the classroom-repo epic (coding-6aa). First boot is slow because the devcontainer installs tooling + npm deps + Playwright, and 'supabase start' pulls ~1GB of Supabase service images. Pre-bake a published image so attendees just pull it. Target environment: local VS Code Dev Containers (NOT Codespaces), so the only viable approach is a published image + tarball-baked Supabase images (Codespaces prebuilds are not an option here).

## Design
1. Dockerfile: build a tooling image (base + node/python + supabase CLI + nsjail; optionally bake 'npm ci' deps + 'playwright install').
2. .github/workflows/<build>.yml: on CI (real docker daemon available):
   - build the tooling image;
   - pin Supabase service image versions (supabase/config.toml), 'supabase start' to pull them, then 'docker save' those images to supabase-images.tar;
   - COPY supabase-images.tar into the published image;
   - push image to ghcr.io/jdelfino/coding-tool-devcontainer:<tag> (public; free for public repos).
3. devcontainer.json: switch from base image + features to "image": "ghcr.io/jdelfino/...". On boot, postCreate does 'docker load -i supabase-images.tar' (local, fast) before 'supabase start' (now offline).

## Notes / limits
- Publishing requires maintainer ghcr permissions / CI; can't be fully verified from an agent session (no registry round-trip, no real devcontainer rebuild).
- Tarball roughly doubles on-disk image size; acceptable for class use.

## Discovered-from
coding-6aa (classroom teaching repo epic).
